import os

BASE_DIR = os.path.abspath(os.path.dirname(__file__))


class Config:
    DATABASE = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, "chat.db")
    DEBUG = False
    TESTING = False
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY", "dev-secret-key-change-in-production")
    JWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS = 24
    SESSION_EXPIRATION_DAYS = 7


class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = True


class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING = True
    DATABASE = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, "test_chat.db")


class ProductionConfig(Config):
    DEBUG = False
    SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get("SECRET_KEY")
    if not SECRET_KEY:
        raise ValueError("SECRET_KEY environment variable is required in production")

import sqlite3
import os
from flask import current_app, g


def get_db():
    if "db" not in g:
        db_path = current_app.config["DATABASE"]
        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(db_path), exist_ok=True)
        g.db = sqlite3.connect(
            db_path,
            detect_types=sqlite3.PARSE_DECLTYPES,
        )
        g.db.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
        g.db.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;")
    return g.db


def close_db(e=None):
    db = g.pop("db", None)
    if db is not None:
        db.close()


def init_db():
    db = get_db()
    with current_app.open_resource("schema.sql") as f:
        db.executescript(f.read().decode("utf-8"))
    db.commit()


def init_app(app):
    app.teardown_appcontext(close_db)
    with app.app_context():
        init_db()

import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(__file__))

from flask import Flask
from flask_cors import CORS

from config import DevelopmentConfig
from database import init_app


def create_app(config_class=DevelopmentConfig):
    app = Flask(__name__)
    app.config.from_object(config_class)

    CORS(app, resources={r"/api/*": {"origins": "*"}})

    init_app(app)

    from routes import auth, chat, messages
    app.register_blueprint(auth.bp, url_prefix="/api/auth")
    app.register_blueprint(chat.bp, url_prefix="/api/chat")
    app.register_blueprint(messages.bp, url_prefix="/api/messages")

    @app.route("/api/health")
    def health():
        return {"status": "ok"}

    return app


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = create_app()
    app.run(debug=True, host="0.0.0.0", port=5000)

import pytest
import os
import sys
import tempfile
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(__file__)))

os.environ["FLASK_ENV"] = "testing"

from app import create_app
from config import TestingConfig
from database import init_db
from models import User, Session, ChatRoom, Message


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = create_app(TestingConfig)
    with app.app_context():
        init_db()
    yield app


@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return app.test_client()


@pytest.fixture
def db(app):
    from database import get_db
    return get_db()


class TestUserModel:
    def test_create_user(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            assert user.id is not None
            assert user.username == "testuser"
            assert user.email == "test@example.com"

    def test_get_by_username(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            user = User.get_by_username("testuser")
            assert user is not None
            assert user.email == "test@example.com"

    def test_get_by_id(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            fetched = User.get_by_id(user.id)
            assert fetched is not None
            assert fetched.username == "testuser"

    def test_verify_password(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            assert User.verify_password(user, "password123")
            assert not User.verify_password(user, "wrongpassword")


class TestSessionModel:
    def test_create_session(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            session = Session.create(user.id)
            assert session.id is not None
            assert session.token is not None
            assert not session.is_expired()

    def test_get_by_token(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            session = Session.create(user.id)
            fetched = Session.get_by_token(session.token)
            assert fetched is not None
            assert fetched.user_id == user.id

    def test_expired_session(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            session = Session.create(user.id)
            session.expires_at = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=1)
            from database import get_db
            db = get_db()
            db.execute("UPDATE sessions SET expires_at = ? WHERE id = ?",
                      (session.expires_at, session.id))
            db.commit()
            assert Session.get_by_token(session.token) is None


class TestChatRoomModel:
    def test_create_room(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            room = ChatRoom.create("General", "General discussion", user.id)
            assert room.id is not None
            assert room.name == "General"

    def test_get_all_rooms(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            ChatRoom.create("Room1", "First room", user.id)
            ChatRoom.create("Room2", "Second room", user.id)
            rooms = ChatRoom.get_all()
            assert len(rooms) == 2


class TestMessageModel:
    def test_create_message(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            room = ChatRoom.create("General", "General discussion", user.id)
            message = Message.create(room.id, user.id, "Hello world!")
            assert message.id is not None
            assert message.content == "Hello world!"

    def test_get_messages_by_room(self, app):
        with app.app_context():
            user = User.create("testuser", "test@example.com", "password123")
            room = ChatRoom.create("General", "General discussion", user.id)
            Message.create(room.id, user.id, "Message 1")
            Message.create(room.id, user.id, "Message 2")
            messages = Message.get_by_room(room.id)
            assert len(messages) == 2


class TestAuthEndpoints:
    def test_register(self, client):
        response = client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "newuser",
            "email": "new@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        assert response.status_code == 201
        data = response.get_json()
        assert "token" in data
        assert data["user"]["username"] == "newuser"

    def test_register_duplicate(self, client):
        client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "newuser",
            "email": "new@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        response = client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "newuser",
            "email": "another@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        assert response.status_code == 409

    def test_login(self, client):
        client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "loginuser",
            "email": "login@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        response = client.post("/api/auth/login", json={
            "username": "loginuser",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = response.get_json()
        assert "token" in data

    def test_login_invalid_credentials(self, client):
        response = client.post("/api/auth/login", json={
            "username": "nonexistent",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_get_current_user(self, client):
        reg_response = client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "currentuser",
            "email": "current@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        token = reg_response.get_json()["token"]

        response = client.get("/api/auth/me", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert response.get_json()["username"] == "currentuser"

    def test_unauthorized_access(self, client):
        response = client.get("/api/auth/me")
        assert response.status_code == 401


class TestChatEndpoints:
    def test_list_rooms(self, client):
        response = client.get("/api/chat/rooms")
        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert isinstance(response.get_json(), list)

    def test_create_room(self, client):
        reg_response = client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "roomuser",
            "email": "room@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        token = reg_response.get_json()["token"]

        response = client.post("/api/chat/rooms",
            json={"name": "TestRoom", "description": "Test description"},
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
        )
        assert response.status_code == 201
        assert response.get_json()["name"] == "TestRoom"


class TestMessageEndpoints:
    def test_send_and_get_messages(self, client):
        reg_response = client.post("/api/auth/register", json={
            "username": "msguser",
            "email": "msg@example.com",
            "password": "password123"
        })
        token = reg_response.get_json()["token"]
        user_id = reg_response.get_json()["user"]["id"]

        room_response = client.post("/api/chat/rooms",
            json={"name": "MsgRoom", "description": "Message room"},
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
        )
        room_id = room_response.get_json()["id"]

        send_response = client.post(f"/api/messages/rooms/{room_id}/messages",
            json={"content": "Hello!"},
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
        )
        assert send_response.status_code == 201

        get_response = client.get(f"/api/messages/rooms/{room_id}/messages")
        assert get_response.status_code == 200
        messages = get_response.get_json()
        assert len(messages) == 1
        assert messages[0]["content"] == "Hello!"

summary: Created backend project structure with SQLite database, models for users, sessions/auth tokens, chat rooms, and persisted messages. Includes Flask app with REST API endpoints for authentication, chat rooms, and messaging. Added comprehensive tests.
